stories.title: Trump declares US-Iran peace deal complete
stories.description: Pakistan's PM Sharif, who mediated the talks, said both sides agreed to end all military operations. A formal signing is set for June 19.

stories.title: Skydiving plane crash kills all 12 on board in Missouri
stories.description: The plane had just taken off from Butler Memorial Airport when it lost power, stalled, and went down nose-first, the acting airport manager said.

stories.title: DR Congo Ebola cases reach 782 as virus spreads to two more health zones
stories.description: The Bundibugyo strain outbreak, declared May 15, now spans 31 health zones with contact tracing far below the target needed for containment.

stories.title: Trump aides fear reporters obtained Situation Room tapes
stories.description: Axios reports White House officials suspect Haberman and Swan acquired secret audio from sensitive meetings for their book "Regime Change," due June 23.

stories.title: Sen. McConnell hospitalized Sunday in second stay this year
stories.description: The 84-year-old Kentucky Republican is "receiving excellent care," his spokesperson said. No reason for admission was given.

stories.title: Sweden scrambles jets to intercept Russian aircraft twice in one day
stories.description: The Friday interceptions over the Baltic Sea came a day after Sweden's defense commission warned Russia may test NATO's cohesion soon.

stories.title: Trump marks 80th birthday with UFC fights on White House lawn
stories.description: The president hosts UFC Freedom 250 on the South Lawn as polls show growing public concern over his health and mental acuity.

stories.title: UN reports Ukraine's May civilian toll highest in 4 years
stories.description: At least 274 civilians were killed and 1,763 injured, a 93% increase over May 2025, according to UN monitors.

stories.title: UAE agreed to unlock billions for Iran, sources say
stories.description: Reuters reported that the UAE agreed to release up to $10 billion to halt Iranian attacks, though the UAE categorically denied the claims.

stories.title: Trump opposes FISA renewal without voting bill attached
stories.description: The president said Sunday he won't back restoring the lapsed surveillance authority unless Congress pairs it with the Save America Act.

stories.title: China vows retaliation over Pentagon blacklisting of Alibaba, BYD and Baidu
stories.description: Beijing's Commerce Ministry warned Saturday it will "inevitably retaliate resolutely and forcefully" over the addition of major tech firms to the U.S. military companies list.

stories.title: State Attorneys General open multistate investigation into OpenAI
stories.description: New York's attorney general served OpenAI with a sweeping subpoena on behalf of a coalition of states, targeting user data practices and impact on minors.

stories.title: Charlie Javice seeks Trump pardon for JPMorgan fraud conviction
stories.description: The Frank founder, sentenced to 85 months for fabricating customer data to sell her startup for $175 million, has reached out to Trump allies.

stories.title: DOJ approves Paramount's $111 billion Warner Bros. Discovery merger
stories.description: The deal cleared federal antitrust review without any required divestitures or concessions, though state attorneys general may still sue to block it.

stories.title: Elon Musk becomes the world’s first trillionaire after SpaceX IPO
stories.description: The rocket and satellite company raised $75 billion by selling shares at $135 each, valuing SpaceX at $1.77 trillion at its Nasdaq debut.

stories.title: Trump calls Putin and Zelenskyy on eve of G7 summit
stories.description: The president held back-to-back calls on his 80th birthday, discussing Ukraine peace efforts and a potential Iran deal hours before G7 talks begin.

stories.title: EU says it has proof China trained Russian troops fighting in Ukraine
stories.description: European intelligence confirmed that hundreds of Russian soldiers trained at Chinese military bases before deploying to the front lines.

stories.title: Pentagon releases third batch of declassified UFO files
stories.description: The latest tranche includes 72 documents, videos, and NASA audio recordings spanning back to 1949, with new firsthand accounts of luminous "orbs."

stories.title: Trump rebukes Israel after Beirut strike threatens Iran deal
stories.description: Israel struck Hezbollah targets in Beirut on Sunday, killing at least three, as Trump called the attack a threat to an imminent U.S.-Iran agreement.

stories.title: Bezos and Amodei offer clashing visions of AI's job impact
stories.description: AI-linked U.S. layoffs hit a record monthly high in May even as Bezos predicts the technology will create labor shortages, not eliminate jobs.

stories.title: U.S. plans to cut fighter jets, warships for NATO in Europe
stories.description: A New York Times report details sweeping reductions to aircraft, submarines, and carriers available for European defense operations.

stories.title: Kennedy Center confirms full removal of Trump's name
stories.description: The administration filed a court certification Saturday confirming compliance with a judge's order that the renaming was unlawful.

stories.title: Bezos' AI startup Prometheus raises $12B at $41B valuation
stories.description: The industrial AI company closed its Series B backed by JPMorgan, BlackRock, and Goldman Sachs, bringing total funding to $18.2 billion.

stories.title: Pentagon locked down after air quality alert triggers hazmat response
stories.description: Emergency crews in full chemical protective gear responded Thursday after internal systems flagged an air quality concern at the military headquarters.

stories.title: Iran mines nuclear tunnels as US deal signing looms
stories.description: Tehran collapsed tunnels and mined entrances to seal off its uranium stockpile, CNN reported, as Trump says a deal could be signed Sunday.

stories.title: ECB hikes rates for first time in 3 years as Iran war fuels inflation
stories.description: The deposit rate rises to 2.25% as eurozone inflation hits 3.2%, with markets pricing in roughly even odds of another increase in September.

stories.title: Supreme Court blocks Alabama nitrogen gas execution
stories.description: The 6-3 ruling upheld a lower court's finding that nitrogen hypoxia violates the Eighth Amendment's ban on cruel and unusual punishment.

stories.title: Trump says US strike killed Tren de Aragua leader
stories.description: The president announced the killing of Niño Guerrero, who had been indicted on terrorism and racketeering charges and carried a $5 million bounty.

stories.title: OpenAI considers drastic token price cuts to fend off Anthropic
stories.description: The move, reported by The Wall Street Journal, comes as both AI giants prepare for IPOs and enterprise customers push back on soaring costs.

stories.title: Boelter pleads guilty to killing Minnesota lawmaker Hortman
stories.description: The plea deal calls for two life sentences plus 40 years after federal prosecutors agreed not to seek the death penalty.

stories.title: Trump admin blocks foreign access to Anthropic's Fable 5, Mythos 5
stories.description: Commerce Secretary Lutnick ordered export restrictions on Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5, barring all foreign users days after the models launched.

stories.title: US strikes destroy water reservoirs in Iran, drawing war crime accusations
stories.description: Iran says US airstrikes on Sirik cut drinking water to thousands, as both sides trade fire in a second day of renewed hostilities.

stories.title: Gates tells Congress Epstein tried to blackmail him
stories.description: The Microsoft co-founder testified before the House Oversight Committee, saying Epstein used knowledge of his affairs to pressure him.

stories.title: Judge indefinitely blocks Trump's $1.8B anti-weaponization fund
stories.description: The ruling replaces a temporary order that was set to expire Friday, and seeks a sworn declaration that the administration won't revive the program.

stories.title: Hegseth warns Cuba against acquiring weapons during Guantanamo visit
stories.description: The defense secretary told Havana that seeking arms capable of reaching the base or the U.S. homeland would invite a confrontation it "could not withstand."

stories.title: IAEA board passes resolution demanding Iran account for uranium stockpile
stories.description: The U.S.-backed measure requires Tehran to disclose the status of enriched uranium at sites bombed during last year's conflict and grant inspectors access.

stories.title: Trump calls Iran's leaked deal terms fake, slams drone attack
stories.description: The president dismissed a 14-point draft memorandum published by Iranian media and accused Tehran of a drone strike on Indian ships near Hormuz.

stories.title: US consumer prices rose 4.2% in May, fastest pace in 3 years
stories.description: The Iran war-driven energy surge pushed headline CPI past 4% for the first time since 2023, though a softer core reading eased fears of broader inflation.

stories.title: IRGC claims drone strike on US Fifth Fleet in Bahrain
stories.description: Iran says it attacked the US naval headquarters hours after American strikes hit Iranian military sites in a rapid cycle of retaliation.

stories.title: Trump and allies push to void his two impeachments
stories.description: The effort to formally expunge both first-term House impeachment votes from the record would wait until after the November midterm elections.

stories.title: Fired Navy admiral advances to runoff for Mace's SC House seat
stories.description: Nancy Lacore, dismissed by Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, will face Mac Deford in a June 23 Democratic runoff for South Carolina's 1st District.

stories.title: Energy Secretary Wright says Hormuz recovery months away
stories.description: Ship traffic through the strait is rising "very meaningfully," but Wright warned that full normalization of energy flows will take many months.

stories.title: Russia's war in Ukraine surpasses World War I in duration
stories.description: The full-scale invasion reached 1,569 days on Wednesday, outlasting the First World War as the Trump administration's June peace deadline passed without resolution.

stories.title: LePage wins Maine GOP nod in key House battleground
stories.description: The former governor ran unopposed for the 2nd District seat being vacated by retiring Rep. Jared Golden.

stories.title: Belfast rocked by riots after Sudanese man charged in stabbing
stories.description: Masked protesters torched vehicles and blocked roads Tuesday night as the suspect, a refugee granted UK asylum in 2023, was charged with attempted murder.

stories.title: Trump administration appeals ruling that struck down $100,000 H-1B visa fee
stories.description: The Justice Department filed a notice of appeal Thursday, escalating a legal fight over the fee a federal judge called an unlawful tax.

stories.title: Platner wins Maine Democratic Senate primary, will face Collins in fall
stories.description: The scandal-plagued oyster farmer and veteran secured the nomination decisively, setting up a marquee race for Senate control.

stories.title: House passes Faster Labor Contracts Act as 20 Republicans break ranks
stories.description: The bipartisan bill would force employers to begin union contract talks within 10 days and impose binding arbitration if negotiations stall.

stories.title: White House defends Cuba sanctions after UN warns children are dying
stories.description: The Trump administration said sanctions target the Cuban regime, rejecting UN calls to lift measures that officials say are causing "widespread harm."

stories.title: Graham wins South Carolina GOP Senate primary, avoids runoff
stories.description: The four-term incumbent fended off businessman Mark Lynch and four other challengers Tuesday in his bid for a fifth term.

stories.title: Anthropic launches Claude Fable 5, its first public Mythos-class model
stories.description: The AI model uses automatic safety classifiers that route sensitive queries to a less powerful system, with mandatory 30-day data retention.

stories.title: Iran denies Trump's claim of direct contact as strikes intensify
stories.description: Tehran called Trump's assertion that Iranian officials asked him to stop bombing "completely false," as tit-for-tat strikes entered a second day.

stories.title: Becerra, Hilton advance to California governor race
stories.description: Republican Steve Hilton clinched the second spot Tuesday, setting up a November showdown with Democrat Xavier Becerra to succeed Gov. Gavin Newsom.

stories.title: Social Security trust fund now projected to run dry in 2032
stories.description: The 2026 trustees report moves the OASI depletion date up a year, meaning retirees could face a 22% benefit cut as early as late 2032.

stories.title: Trump claims US secretly took oil from Iran; Energy Secretary says he was unaware
stories.description: Energy Secretary Chris Wright told Congress he had no knowledge of the operation Trump described as extracting "millions of barrels every night."

stories.title: NASA names four astronauts for Artemis III lunar mission
stories.description: Bresnik, Parmitano, Douglas, and Rubio will fly a 2027 Earth-orbit test flight critical to returning humans to the moon.

stories.title: Houthis strike two ships after banning Israeli Red Sea navigation
stories.description: The attacks enforce a declared ban on Israeli-linked vessels, threatening Saudi Arabia's last functioning oil export route as the Strait of Hormuz remains closed.

stories.title: Trump says he may not renew USMCA trade deal
stories.description: The president's remarks come weeks before the July 1 review deadline, as Canada and Mexico both push for a 16-year extension.

stories.title: Israel strikes Tyre, killing 8, defying Iran warning
stories.description: The IDF issued its first evacuation order covering Tyre's Christian quarter as airstrikes hit the southern Lebanese city Tuesday.

stories.title: Drone boat rescues Apache crew in military first after Strait of Hormuz crash
stories.description: A U.S. military sea drone conducted its first-ever water rescue after an AH-64 Apache went down off Oman's coast Monday evening.

stories.title: Trump signs $70B Secure America Act funding ICE, CBP
stories.description: The law locks in immigration enforcement funding through 2029, ending a months-long standoff that triggered the longest government shutdown in U.S. history.

stories.title: China plans $295B data center buildout to rival U.S. in AI
stories.description: Beijing is drafting a blueprint for interconnected computing hubs nationwide, funded largely through sovereign debt over the next five years.

stories.title: Philippines earthquake death toll rises to 37 as rescuers search for survivors
stories.description: The 7.8-magnitude quake struck off Mindanao on Monday, triggering landslides, building collapses, and tsunami waves across the region.

stories.title: Cuba calls Trump sanctions a 'pretext' for military action
stories.description: Cuba's top envoy said the indictment of Raúl Castro and new sanctions aim to build U.S. public support for an invasion of the island.

stories.title: Watchdog finds flawed data, security gaps in IRS-ICE deal
stories.description: TIGTA's report documents matching errors and ICE's failure to safeguard taxpayer information shared under the controversial 2025 agreement.

stories.title: Senate Democrats seek probe into Duffy's corporate-funded road trip
stories.description: Six senators asked the DOT inspector general to investigate the transportation secretary's family video series bankrolled by regulated companies.

stories.title: US warns Europe to tighten Ebola travel curbs or face restrictions
stories.description: The Trump administration says Europe's inaction on Ebola screening could trigger limits on European travelers ahead of the World Cup.

stories.title: State Department to offer $750 fast-track visa interviews
stories.description: A pilot program launching July 1 lets B-1/B-2 visa applicants pay $750 on top of the standard $185 fee to secure an interview within 10 business days.

stories.title: 6.1 earthquake off Cuba sends tremors across Florida
stories.description: The quake struck around 2 p.m. Monday about 65 miles from Mantua, Cuba, with shaking reported from the Keys to Jacksonville.

stories.title: MAGA Inc. plans $1M-per-person fundraiser before White House UFC event
stories.description: Trump's super PAC is scheduled to hold the dinner Saturday at his Virginia golf club, one day before UFC Freedom 250 on the South Lawn.

stories.title: DOJ moves to strip citizenship from 17 naturalized Americans
stories.description: The individuals are accused of hiding serious crimes including child sexual abuse, drug distribution, and fraud during the naturalization process.

stories.title: Germany and France abandon joint fighter jet program
stories.description: The leaders agreed the core manned aircraft of the €100 billion FCAS project is dead after Airbus and Dassault failed to resolve their dispute.

stories.title: Trump to ask Supreme Court to revive $475M CNN suit
stories.description: His lawyers requested a 60-day extension to file a petition challenging lower courts' dismissal of the "Big Lie" defamation case.

stories.title: Homan vows major ICE surge in New York City
stories.description: The border czar said he reviewed an operational plan to flood the city with agents after Gov. Hochul signed laws curbing ICE cooperation.

stories.title: Ukraine recaptured over 600 sq km in 2026, top commander says
stories.description: Commander-in-Chief Syrskyi said Ukraine recaptured 100 sq km more than it lost in May alone, signaling a continued shift in battlefield momentum.

stories.title: Trump formally nominates Todd Blanche as attorney general
stories.description: The former Trump defense lawyer, acting AG since April, faces an uncertain confirmation path with slim Republican margins on the Judiciary Committee.

stories.title: Google orders 3 million TPUs from Intel as Nvidia eyes foundry partnership
stories.description: The massive 2028 chip order sent Intel shares surging in premarket trading and signals growing confidence in the company's foundry revival.

stories.title: Raman passes Pratt for 2nd in LA mayoral primary
stories.description: Los Angeles City Councilmember Nithya Raman will face incumbent Mayor Karen Bass in the Nov. 3 runoff after overtaking Spencer Pratt on Sunday.

stories.title: Trump net approval hits -19.1 as gas prices erode base
stories.description: Polling averages show Trump less popular than Biden or his own first term at this point, with 71% disapproving of his economic handling.

stories.title: US doctor discharged from Berlin hospital after Ebola recovery
stories.description: Dr. Peter Stafford contracted the Bundibugyo strain while performing surgery in Congo and was treated with experimental antiviral therapies.

stories.title: Germany proposes €70B NATO military aid pledge for Ukraine
stories.description: The proposal, discussed ahead of NATO's July summit in Ankara, would formalize long-term support and improve transparency on allied contributions.

stories.title: Allies to press Trump for Hormuz mine-clearing nod at G7
stories.description: A UK-France-led coalition of 15 nations is operationally ready to clear mines from the Strait of Hormuz once a US-Iran deal is reached.

stories.title: Israeli troops kill 7-month-old Palestinian baby in West Bank
stories.description: The IDF said soldiers fired at a vehicle they perceived accelerating toward them and acknowledged the victims were "uninvolved civilians."

stories.title: Hegseth warns Europe faces 'invasion' of ideologies at D-Day ceremony
stories.description: Speaking at the 82nd anniversary of the Normandy landings, the defense secretary urged European allies to bolster their own defenses amid U.S. troop withdrawals.

stories.title: Sam Bankman-Fried files formal pardon application from prison
stories.description: The convicted FTX co-founder told Fox Business he "absolutely" wants clemency, despite the White House repeatedly saying Trump won't grant it.

stories.title: US measles cases top 2,000 in just five months
stories.description: The CDC confirmed 2,030 cases so far in 2026, reaching the milestone months faster than last year, when it took until late December.

stories.title: Ukraine launches massive drone attack on St. Petersburg
stories.description: Russia says it intercepted 376 drones as St. Petersburg's governor urged residents to stay indoors after Putin rejected Zelensky's peace proposal.

stories.title: US-Iran war hits 100 days with peace talks stalled
stories.description: Trump claims Iran agreed to forgo nuclear weapons, but critics say none of the war's five stated objectives have been achieved.

stories.title: Pentagon raises Israel spy threat to highest level
stories.description: The Defense Intelligence Agency elevated Israel's counterintelligence designation to "critical" over concerns about surveillance of senior U.S. officials.

stories.title: Judge orders ex-CIA officer with $40M in gold to stay jailed
stories.description: A Virginia magistrate found David Rush poses a flight risk, calling him uniquely positioned to evade authorities given his intelligence background.

stories.title: Trump denies promising 'no new wars,' defends Iran conflict
stories.description: In a "Meet the Press" interview airing Sunday, the president rejected claims he pledged to avoid military conflict during his campaign.

stories.title: Iran's World Cup squad granted US visas, White House says
stories.description: The decision resolves weeks of uncertainty just 10 days before the team's opening match against New Zealand in Los Angeles.

stories.title: Putin rejects Zelenskyy's proposal for direct talks
stories.description: Russia's president dismissed the call for face-to-face negotiations, saying Moscow wants a comprehensive settlement, not a temporary ceasefire.

stories.title: Trump walks out of NBC interview over election fraud clash
stories.description: He called anchor Kristen Welker "either crooked or stupid" during a heated exchange about his unsubstantiated California election claims.

stories.title: US says 129 ships diverted under naval blockade of Iran
stories.description: CENTCOM reported Friday that six vessels have been disabled for noncompliance as the blockade nears its eighth week.

stories.title: NASA orders ISS crew to shelter amid worsening air leak
stories.description: Astronauts donned spacesuits and boarded their SpaceX capsule Friday as a years-long leak in the Russian segment escalated.

stories.title: Trump's top AI adviser Krishnan to leave White House
stories.description: Sriram Krishnan, who shaped the administration's deregulatory AI agenda, plans to depart his senior policy role by the end of June.

stories.title: US counts nearly 1,000 Hormuz transits since ceasefire
stories.description: CENTCOM's tally far exceeds the roughly 650 crossings detected by private ship-tracking services, exposing a major data gap in the world's top oil corridor.

stories.title: US adds 172,000 jobs in May, far exceeding forecasts
stories.description: The unemployment rate held at 4.3% as payroll growth more than doubled some Wall Street estimates, complicating the case for Fed rate cuts.

stories.title: Judge blocks Trump effort to tie SNAP funds to policy conditions
stories.description: A federal judge sided with 20 states, issuing a preliminary injunction against attaching immigration and gender-related requirements to food aid funding.

stories.title: Iran touts $25B nuclear deal with Russia as US talks stall
stories.description: Iran's ambassador revealed the scope of the Hormoz Nuclear Power Plant project as Tehran declared no progress in peace negotiations with Washington.

stories.title: House panel requires Pentagon to explain officer firings
stories.description: The House Armed Services Committee adopted a measure mandating the defense secretary report to Congress within five days of removing a senior officer.

stories.title: Trump pardons ex-Rep. Stephen Buyer for insider trading
stories.description: The former Indiana congressman was convicted of securities fraud for illegal stock trades made while consulting for T-Mobile during its Sprint merger.

stories.title: Pentagon set to cancel Tomahawk missile sale to Germany
stories.description: The expected move would end Germany's bid to buy up to 400 cruise missiles directly, deepening a rift between Washington and Berlin over European defense.

stories.title: Hezbollah rejects U.S.-brokered ceasefire as strikes kill 4 in Lebanon
stories.description: The group called the deal "absurd" and demanded a full Israeli withdrawal, one day after Israel and Lebanon's government agreed to a new framework.

stories.title: Trump holds farmer roundtable in first Wisconsin visit since reelection
stories.description: At his first Wisconsin visit of his second term, the president campaigned for a vulnerable House Republican while promising relief for farmers.

stories.title: John Bolton to plead guilty in classified documents case
stories.description: The former national security adviser agreed to a single charge and a $2.25 million fine, potentially avoiding prison on the original 18-count indictment.

stories.title: US job cuts hit highest May total since 2020 as Iran war fallout spreads
stories.description: The EU warned 1.3 million jobs are at risk in energy-intensive industries, while US and UK layoffs accelerated sharply.

stories.title: Trump signs memo to speed AI use in military operations
stories.description: The directive orders agencies to adopt AI from multiple vendors while barring its use for illegal surveillance or censoring speech.

stories.title: US strike on alleged drug boat kills 2, pushing toll past 200
stories.description: The June 3 strike in the eastern Pacific is the latest in a campaign that has hit more than 60 vessels since September 2025.

stories.title: Khamenei says US, Israel shifted to 'hybrid war' after battlefield defeat
stories.description: Iran's supreme leader accused adversaries of spreading fear and division, urging national unity at the 37th anniversary of Ayatollah Khomeini's death.

stories.title: Federal judge strikes down Trump immigration freeze on 39 countries
stories.description: U.S. District Judge McConnell ruled Friday that USCIS unlawfully halted green card, work permit, and asylum processing for nationals of travel-ban countries.

stories.title: All 27 EU members approve opening accession talks with Ukraine, Moldova
stories.description: Hungary lifted its long-standing veto after reaching a minority rights deal with Kyiv, ending a two-year deadlock on membership negotiations.

stories.title: CENTCOM calls Iran's claim of striking US destroyer a lie
stories.description: Iran said it hit a US Navy destroyer's command center in the Gulf of Oman; CENTCOM said the claim is false.

stories.title: Trump says he wants Pulte to fire intelligence staff
stories.description: The president told the Wall Street Journal he views ODNI as "unneeded and possibly too extensive," escalating his overhaul of spy agencies.

stories.title: Kim Jong Un calls for 'exponential' nuclear expansion
stories.description: North Korea's leader toured a newly operational nuclear material production facility, saying output capacity has more than doubled in five years.

stories.title: Rubio says Greenland is part of Denmark 'for now'
stories.description: The secretary of state told lawmakers that U.S. talks with Denmark and Greenland on collective defense are "in a good place."

stories.title: Bondi told lawmakers Blanche ran Epstein files release
stories.description: The 111-page transcript shows the former attorney general invoking executive privilege on any Trump conversations about the case.

stories.title: Pentagon watchdog to lead oversight of Iran war
stories.description: Inspector General Platte B. Moring III will coordinate reviews across the Pentagon, State Department, and USAID for Operation Epic Fury.

stories.title: Proactiv, Dr. Pimple Popper acne creams had benzene 15 times FDA limit
stories.description: Bloomberg reported that government lab results showed recalled acne products from Proactiv and SLMD contained benzene levels far exceeding federal safety limits.

stories.title: Senate GOP blocks bid to bar Trump's IRS audit immunity fund
stories.description: Democrats failed to kill the $1.8 billion "Anti-Weaponization Fund" in a 49-50 vote, as the status of Trump's audit immunity remains unresolved.

stories.title: US proposes tariffs on 60 economies over forced labor
stories.description: The sweeping Section 301 action covers more than 99% of U.S. imports and marks a major step in rebuilding tariff authority after the Supreme Court struck down IEEPA levies.

stories.title: Bolivia's defense minister resigns amid weeks of mass protests
stories.description: Marcelo Salinas's departure is the highest-level exit from President Rodrigo Paz's government since unrest erupted in early May.

stories.title: Trump's team refuses financial disclosure in BBC lawsuit
stories.description: His lawyers sought to halt discovery after the BBC subpoenaed records from nearly 400 entities tied to the Trump Revocable Trust.

stories.title: CBS fires Scott Pelley after clash with new '60 Minutes' boss
stories.description: The veteran correspondent was terminated Tuesday after accusing Bari Weiss of "murdering" the iconic newsmagazine during a staff meeting.

stories.title: Supreme Court lets Alabama eliminate a Black-majority district
stories.description: The 6-3 ruling allows a GOP-drawn congressional map for the 2026 midterms, overriding a lower court finding of intentional racial discrimination.

stories.title: White House ousts NSC Europe chief in Rubio power play
stories.description: The departure of Charles McLaughlin signals Secretary of State Rubio's tightening grip over National Security Council staffing and policy.

stories.title: Jeffries says Democrats not focused on Trump impeachment
stories.description: The House minority leader told business leaders the party will prioritize affordability and anti-corruption ahead of the November midterms.

stories.title: George Santos faces federal probe over Kalshi bets on his own SOTU attendance
stories.description: The DOJ and CFTC are investigating whether the disgraced former congressman profited by wagering against his own appearance at the State of the Union.

stories.title: Trump confirms calling Netanyahu 'crazy' in call over Lebanon strikes
stories.description: The president said he was "a little bit perturbed" by Israel's operations against Hezbollah, which he says are undermining U.S.-Iran peace talks.

stories.title: Rubio says Netanyahu's plan to seize 70% of Gaza contradicts US vision
stories.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio told lawmakers Tuesday that Israel's expansion directive is not part of Washington's plan for the territory.

stories.title: Navy veteran Bennett wins NJ-7 primary to face absent Rep. Kean
stories.description: Bennett will challenge Rep. Tom Kean Jr., who has missed over 100 congressional votes during a months-long undisclosed medical absence.

stories.title: Trump strips job protections from 8,000 federal workers
stories.description: An executive order signed Wednesday converts senior career employees to at-will status, removing their right to appeal disciplinary actions or terminations.

stories.title: UNICEF says Iran war delaying lifesaving aid for children
stories.description: The agency warned Tuesday that surging transport costs and Gulf shipping disruptions are forcing it to rely on costly air freight to reach children.

stories.title: US sanctions two rebel commanders over eastern Congo conflict
stories.description: The Treasury Department targeted an M23 intelligence chief and an FDLR special operations commander fueling violence in the DRC.

stories.title: House passes war powers resolution to end Iran war
stories.description: Four Republicans joined Democrats in the 215-208 vote, delivering a bipartisan rebuke to President Trump over the three-month-old conflict.

stories.title: Bahrain bans citizens from traveling to Iran and Iraq
stories.description: The ban, citing security tensions, comes as millions of Shiites prepare for pilgrimage season, drawing accusations of sectarian discrimination.

stories.title: Rubio says five countries may take stranded Afghan allies
stories.description: More than 1,000 Afghans who aided U.S. forces remain stuck at a base in Qatar, but Rubio told senators they won't be resettled in America.

stories.title: Iowa farmer Lahn defeats Trump-backed Feenstra in GOP governor primary
stories.description: Zach Lahn, aligned with RFK Jr.'s MAHA movement, edged Rep. Randy Feenstra by less than a point in Tuesday's five-candidate race.

stories.title: Hegseth blocks Navy officers from admiral promotions
stories.description: The defense secretary removed at least seven captains from a one-star admiral list, including two women and two Black men, reports say.

stories.title: Takaichi urges Iran to restore free passage through Hormuz
stories.description: Japan's PM called on Iran's president to show flexibility in U.S.-Iran talks and reopen the strait to all vessels.

stories.title: Trump calls Vance-Rubio 2028 ticket 'unbeatable'
stories.description: The president's podcast remarks are his most direct praise yet of the pairing, though he still stopped short of a formal endorsement.

stories.title: White House Correspondents' Dinner set for July 24 after shooting postponement
stories.description: The WHCA announced the rescheduled date Tuesday, nearly three months after a gunman disrupted the original April 25 event at the Washington Hilton.

stories.title: State Dept. to cut Africa visa-processing sites from 50 to 20 hubs
stories.description: Secretary of State Rubio approved a directive consolidating consular operations across Africa, forcing applicants in non-hub countries to travel abroad for visas.

stories.title: Dr. Oz helms White House briefing, dodges non-health questions
stories.description: The CMS administrator announced 160 new drugs on the TrumpRx discount platform while deflecting questions outside his portfolio.

stories.title: Pentagon bars journalists from press office, declaring it classified space
stories.description: The designation marks the latest escalation in a yearlong campaign to restrict media access to the Defense Department.

stories.title: Frederiksen secures third term as Denmark's PM with new centre-left coalition
stories.description: The deal ends over two months of deadlocked talks following March's fragmented snap election, which saw 12 parties win seats in parliament.

stories.title: Trump endorses Colombia presidential candidate De La Espriella
stories.description: The endorsement came via Truth Social on Tuesday as the conservative outsider prepares to face leftist senator Ivan Cepeda in a June 21 runoff.

stories.title: Appeals court rules Pentagon transgender troop ban illegal
stories.description: A divided D.C. Circuit panel ruled the Pentagon's transgender military ban illegal but allowed the military to keep barring new enlistees.

stories.title: Anthropic confidentially files for IPO after $965B valuation
stories.description: The Claude AI maker submitted a draft S-1 to the SEC on Monday, joining SpaceX and OpenAI in a historic wave of tech IPOs expected this year.

top.title: Trump declares US-Iran peace deal complete
top.description: Pakistan's PM Sharif, who mediated the talks, said both sides agreed to end all military operations. A formal signing is set for June 19.

top.title: Skydiving plane crash kills all 12 on board in Missouri
top.description: The plane had just taken off from Butler Memorial Airport when it lost power, stalled, and went down nose-first, the acting airport manager said.

top.title: DR Congo Ebola cases reach 782 as virus spreads to two more health zones
top.description: The Bundibugyo strain outbreak, declared May 15, now spans 31 health zones with contact tracing far below the target needed for containment.

top.title: Trump aides fear reporters obtained Situation Room tapes
top.description: Axios reports White House officials suspect Haberman and Swan acquired secret audio from sensitive meetings for their book "Regime Change," due June 23.

top.title: Sen. McConnell hospitalized Sunday in second stay this year
top.description: The 84-year-old Kentucky Republican is "receiving excellent care," his spokesperson said. No reason for admission was given.

top.title: Sweden scrambles jets to intercept Russian aircraft twice in one day
top.description: The Friday interceptions over the Baltic Sea came a day after Sweden's defense commission warned Russia may test NATO's cohesion soon.

top.title: Trump marks 80th birthday with UFC fights on White House lawn
top.description: The president hosts UFC Freedom 250 on the South Lawn as polls show growing public concern over his health and mental acuity.

top.title: UN reports Ukraine's May civilian toll highest in 4 years
top.description: At least 274 civilians were killed and 1,763 injured, a 93% increase over May 2025, according to UN monitors.

top.title: UAE agreed to unlock billions for Iran, sources say
top.description: Reuters reported that the UAE agreed to release up to $10 billion to halt Iranian attacks, though the UAE categorically denied the claims.

top.title: Trump opposes FISA renewal without voting bill attached
top.description: The president said Sunday he won't back restoring the lapsed surveillance authority unless Congress pairs it with the Save America Act.

top.title: China vows retaliation over Pentagon blacklisting of Alibaba, BYD and Baidu
top.description: Beijing's Commerce Ministry warned Saturday it will "inevitably retaliate resolutely and forcefully" over the addition of major tech firms to the U.S. military companies list.

top.title: State Attorneys General open multistate investigation into OpenAI
top.description: New York's attorney general served OpenAI with a sweeping subpoena on behalf of a coalition of states, targeting user data practices and impact on minors.

top.title: Charlie Javice seeks Trump pardon for JPMorgan fraud conviction
top.description: The Frank founder, sentenced to 85 months for fabricating customer data to sell her startup for $175 million, has reached out to Trump allies.

top.title: DOJ approves Paramount's $111 billion Warner Bros. Discovery merger
top.description: The deal cleared federal antitrust review without any required divestitures or concessions, though state attorneys general may still sue to block it.

top.title: Elon Musk becomes the world’s first trillionaire after SpaceX IPO
top.description: The rocket and satellite company raised $75 billion by selling shares at $135 each, valuing SpaceX at $1.77 trillion at its Nasdaq debut.

top.title: Trump calls Putin and Zelenskyy on eve of G7 summit
top.description: The president held back-to-back calls on his 80th birthday, discussing Ukraine peace efforts and a potential Iran deal hours before G7 talks begin.

top.title: EU says it has proof China trained Russian troops fighting in Ukraine
top.description: European intelligence confirmed that hundreds of Russian soldiers trained at Chinese military bases before deploying to the front lines.

top.title: Pentagon releases third batch of declassified UFO files
top.description: The latest tranche includes 72 documents, videos, and NASA audio recordings spanning back to 1949, with new firsthand accounts of luminous "orbs."

top.title: Trump rebukes Israel after Beirut strike threatens Iran deal
top.description: Israel struck Hezbollah targets in Beirut on Sunday, killing at least three, as Trump called the attack a threat to an imminent U.S.-Iran agreement.

top.title: Bezos and Amodei offer clashing visions of AI's job impact
top.description: AI-linked U.S. layoffs hit a record monthly high in May even as Bezos predicts the technology will create labor shortages, not eliminate jobs.

top.title: U.S. plans to cut fighter jets, warships for NATO in Europe
top.description: A New York Times report details sweeping reductions to aircraft, submarines, and carriers available for European defense operations.

top.title: Kennedy Center confirms full removal of Trump's name
top.description: The administration filed a court certification Saturday confirming compliance with a judge's order that the renaming was unlawful.

top.title: Bezos' AI startup Prometheus raises $12B at $41B valuation
top.description: The industrial AI company closed its Series B backed by JPMorgan, BlackRock, and Goldman Sachs, bringing total funding to $18.2 billion.

top.title: Pentagon locked down after air quality alert triggers hazmat response
top.description: Emergency crews in full chemical protective gear responded Thursday after internal systems flagged an air quality concern at the military headquarters.

top.title: Iran mines nuclear tunnels as US deal signing looms
top.description: Tehran collapsed tunnels and mined entrances to seal off its uranium stockpile, CNN reported, as Trump says a deal could be signed Sunday.

top.title: ECB hikes rates for first time in 3 years as Iran war fuels inflation
top.description: The deposit rate rises to 2.25% as eurozone inflation hits 3.2%, with markets pricing in roughly even odds of another increase in September.

top.title: Supreme Court blocks Alabama nitrogen gas execution
top.description: The 6-3 ruling upheld a lower court's finding that nitrogen hypoxia violates the Eighth Amendment's ban on cruel and unusual punishment.

top.title: Trump says US strike killed Tren de Aragua leader
top.description: The president announced the killing of Niño Guerrero, who had been indicted on terrorism and racketeering charges and carried a $5 million bounty.

top.title: OpenAI considers drastic token price cuts to fend off Anthropic
top.description: The move, reported by The Wall Street Journal, comes as both AI giants prepare for IPOs and enterprise customers push back on soaring costs.

top.title: Boelter pleads guilty to killing Minnesota lawmaker Hortman
top.description: The plea deal calls for two life sentences plus 40 years after federal prosecutors agreed not to seek the death penalty.

top.title: Trump admin blocks foreign access to Anthropic's Fable 5, Mythos 5
top.description: Commerce Secretary Lutnick ordered export restrictions on Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5, barring all foreign users days after the models launched.

top.title: US strikes destroy water reservoirs in Iran, drawing war crime accusations
top.description: Iran says US airstrikes on Sirik cut drinking water to thousands, as both sides trade fire in a second day of renewed hostilities.

top.title: Gates tells Congress Epstein tried to blackmail him
top.description: The Microsoft co-founder testified before the House Oversight Committee, saying Epstein used knowledge of his affairs to pressure him.

top.title: Judge indefinitely blocks Trump's $1.8B anti-weaponization fund
top.description: The ruling replaces a temporary order that was set to expire Friday, and seeks a sworn declaration that the administration won't revive the program.

top.title: Hegseth warns Cuba against acquiring weapons during Guantanamo visit
top.description: The defense secretary told Havana that seeking arms capable of reaching the base or the U.S. homeland would invite a confrontation it "could not withstand."

top.title: IAEA board passes resolution demanding Iran account for uranium stockpile
top.description: The U.S.-backed measure requires Tehran to disclose the status of enriched uranium at sites bombed during last year's conflict and grant inspectors access.

top.title: Trump calls Iran's leaked deal terms fake, slams drone attack
top.description: The president dismissed a 14-point draft memorandum published by Iranian media and accused Tehran of a drone strike on Indian ships near Hormuz.

top.title: US consumer prices rose 4.2% in May, fastest pace in 3 years
top.description: The Iran war-driven energy surge pushed headline CPI past 4% for the first time since 2023, though a softer core reading eased fears of broader inflation.

top.title: IRGC claims drone strike on US Fifth Fleet in Bahrain
top.description: Iran says it attacked the US naval headquarters hours after American strikes hit Iranian military sites in a rapid cycle of retaliation.

top.title: Trump and allies push to void his two impeachments
top.description: The effort to formally expunge both first-term House impeachment votes from the record would wait until after the November midterm elections.

top.title: Fired Navy admiral advances to runoff for Mace's SC House seat
top.description: Nancy Lacore, dismissed by Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, will face Mac Deford in a June 23 Democratic runoff for South Carolina's 1st District.

top.title: Energy Secretary Wright says Hormuz recovery months away
top.description: Ship traffic through the strait is rising "very meaningfully," but Wright warned that full normalization of energy flows will take many months.

top.title: Russia's war in Ukraine surpasses World War I in duration
top.description: The full-scale invasion reached 1,569 days on Wednesday, outlasting the First World War as the Trump administration's June peace deadline passed without resolution.

top.title: LePage wins Maine GOP nod in key House battleground
top.description: The former governor ran unopposed for the 2nd District seat being vacated by retiring Rep. Jared Golden.

top.title: Belfast rocked by riots after Sudanese man charged in stabbing
top.description: Masked protesters torched vehicles and blocked roads Tuesday night as the suspect, a refugee granted UK asylum in 2023, was charged with attempted murder.

top.title: Trump administration appeals ruling that struck down $100,000 H-1B visa fee
top.description: The Justice Department filed a notice of appeal Thursday, escalating a legal fight over the fee a federal judge called an unlawful tax.

top.title: Platner wins Maine Democratic Senate primary, will face Collins in fall
top.description: The scandal-plagued oyster farmer and veteran secured the nomination decisively, setting up a marquee race for Senate control.

top.title: House passes Faster Labor Contracts Act as 20 Republicans break ranks
top.description: The bipartisan bill would force employers to begin union contract talks within 10 days and impose binding arbitration if negotiations stall.

top.title: White House defends Cuba sanctions after UN warns children are dying
top.description: The Trump administration said sanctions target the Cuban regime, rejecting UN calls to lift measures that officials say are causing "widespread harm."

top.title: Graham wins South Carolina GOP Senate primary, avoids runoff
top.description: The four-term incumbent fended off businessman Mark Lynch and four other challengers Tuesday in his bid for a fifth term.

top.title: Anthropic launches Claude Fable 5, its first public Mythos-class model
top.description: The AI model uses automatic safety classifiers that route sensitive queries to a less powerful system, with mandatory 30-day data retention.

top.title: Iran denies Trump's claim of direct contact as strikes intensify
top.description: Tehran called Trump's assertion that Iranian officials asked him to stop bombing "completely false," as tit-for-tat strikes entered a second day.

top.title: Becerra, Hilton advance to California governor race
top.description: Republican Steve Hilton clinched the second spot Tuesday, setting up a November showdown with Democrat Xavier Becerra to succeed Gov. Gavin Newsom.

top.title: Social Security trust fund now projected to run dry in 2032
top.description: The 2026 trustees report moves the OASI depletion date up a year, meaning retirees could face a 22% benefit cut as early as late 2032.

top.title: Trump claims US secretly took oil from Iran; Energy Secretary says he was unaware
top.description: Energy Secretary Chris Wright told Congress he had no knowledge of the operation Trump described as extracting "millions of barrels every night."

top.title: NASA names four astronauts for Artemis III lunar mission
top.description: Bresnik, Parmitano, Douglas, and Rubio will fly a 2027 Earth-orbit test flight critical to returning humans to the moon.

top.title: Houthis strike two ships after banning Israeli Red Sea navigation
top.description: The attacks enforce a declared ban on Israeli-linked vessels, threatening Saudi Arabia's last functioning oil export route as the Strait of Hormuz remains closed.

top.title: Trump says he may not renew USMCA trade deal
top.description: The president's remarks come weeks before the July 1 review deadline, as Canada and Mexico both push for a 16-year extension.

top.title: Israel strikes Tyre, killing 8, defying Iran warning
top.description: The IDF issued its first evacuation order covering Tyre's Christian quarter as airstrikes hit the southern Lebanese city Tuesday.

top.title: Drone boat rescues Apache crew in military first after Strait of Hormuz crash
top.description: A U.S. military sea drone conducted its first-ever water rescue after an AH-64 Apache went down off Oman's coast Monday evening.

top.title: Trump signs $70B Secure America Act funding ICE, CBP
top.description: The law locks in immigration enforcement funding through 2029, ending a months-long standoff that triggered the longest government shutdown in U.S. history.

top.title: China plans $295B data center buildout to rival U.S. in AI
top.description: Beijing is drafting a blueprint for interconnected computing hubs nationwide, funded largely through sovereign debt over the next five years.

top.title: Philippines earthquake death toll rises to 37 as rescuers search for survivors
top.description: The 7.8-magnitude quake struck off Mindanao on Monday, triggering landslides, building collapses, and tsunami waves across the region.

top.title: Cuba calls Trump sanctions a 'pretext' for military action
top.description: Cuba's top envoy said the indictment of Raúl Castro and new sanctions aim to build U.S. public support for an invasion of the island.

top.title: Watchdog finds flawed data, security gaps in IRS-ICE deal
top.description: TIGTA's report documents matching errors and ICE's failure to safeguard taxpayer information shared under the controversial 2025 agreement.

top.title: Senate Democrats seek probe into Duffy's corporate-funded road trip
top.description: Six senators asked the DOT inspector general to investigate the transportation secretary's family video series bankrolled by regulated companies.

top.title: US warns Europe to tighten Ebola travel curbs or face restrictions
top.description: The Trump administration says Europe's inaction on Ebola screening could trigger limits on European travelers ahead of the World Cup.

top.title: State Department to offer $750 fast-track visa interviews
top.description: A pilot program launching July 1 lets B-1/B-2 visa applicants pay $750 on top of the standard $185 fee to secure an interview within 10 business days.

top.title: 6.1 earthquake off Cuba sends tremors across Florida
top.description: The quake struck around 2 p.m. Monday about 65 miles from Mantua, Cuba, with shaking reported from the Keys to Jacksonville.

top.title: MAGA Inc. plans $1M-per-person fundraiser before White House UFC event
top.description: Trump's super PAC is scheduled to hold the dinner Saturday at his Virginia golf club, one day before UFC Freedom 250 on the South Lawn.

top.title: DOJ moves to strip citizenship from 17 naturalized Americans
top.description: The individuals are accused of hiding serious crimes including child sexual abuse, drug distribution, and fraud during the naturalization process.

top.title: Germany and France abandon joint fighter jet program
top.description: The leaders agreed the core manned aircraft of the €100 billion FCAS project is dead after Airbus and Dassault failed to resolve their dispute.

top.title: Trump to ask Supreme Court to revive $475M CNN suit
top.description: His lawyers requested a 60-day extension to file a petition challenging lower courts' dismissal of the "Big Lie" defamation case.

top.title: Homan vows major ICE surge in New York City
top.description: The border czar said he reviewed an operational plan to flood the city with agents after Gov. Hochul signed laws curbing ICE cooperation.

top.title: Ukraine recaptured over 600 sq km in 2026, top commander says
top.description: Commander-in-Chief Syrskyi said Ukraine recaptured 100 sq km more than it lost in May alone, signaling a continued shift in battlefield momentum.

top.title: Trump formally nominates Todd Blanche as attorney general
top.description: The former Trump defense lawyer, acting AG since April, faces an uncertain confirmation path with slim Republican margins on the Judiciary Committee.

top.title: Google orders 3 million TPUs from Intel as Nvidia eyes foundry partnership
top.description: The massive 2028 chip order sent Intel shares surging in premarket trading and signals growing confidence in the company's foundry revival.

top.title: Raman passes Pratt for 2nd in LA mayoral primary
top.description: Los Angeles City Councilmember Nithya Raman will face incumbent Mayor Karen Bass in the Nov. 3 runoff after overtaking Spencer Pratt on Sunday.

top.title: Trump net approval hits -19.1 as gas prices erode base
top.description: Polling averages show Trump less popular than Biden or his own first term at this point, with 71% disapproving of his economic handling.

top.title: US doctor discharged from Berlin hospital after Ebola recovery
top.description: Dr. Peter Stafford contracted the Bundibugyo strain while performing surgery in Congo and was treated with experimental antiviral therapies.

top.title: Germany proposes €70B NATO military aid pledge for Ukraine
top.description: The proposal, discussed ahead of NATO's July summit in Ankara, would formalize long-term support and improve transparency on allied contributions.

top.title: Allies to press Trump for Hormuz mine-clearing nod at G7
top.description: A UK-France-led coalition of 15 nations is operationally ready to clear mines from the Strait of Hormuz once a US-Iran deal is reached.

top.title: Israeli troops kill 7-month-old Palestinian baby in West Bank
top.description: The IDF said soldiers fired at a vehicle they perceived accelerating toward them and acknowledged the victims were "uninvolved civilians."

top.title: Hegseth warns Europe faces 'invasion' of ideologies at D-Day ceremony
top.description: Speaking at the 82nd anniversary of the Normandy landings, the defense secretary urged European allies to bolster their own defenses amid U.S. troop withdrawals.

top.title: Sam Bankman-Fried files formal pardon application from prison
top.description: The convicted FTX co-founder told Fox Business he "absolutely" wants clemency, despite the White House repeatedly saying Trump won't grant it.

top.title: US measles cases top 2,000 in just five months
top.description: The CDC confirmed 2,030 cases so far in 2026, reaching the milestone months faster than last year, when it took until late December.

top.title: Ukraine launches massive drone attack on St. Petersburg
top.description: Russia says it intercepted 376 drones as St. Petersburg's governor urged residents to stay indoors after Putin rejected Zelensky's peace proposal.

top.title: US-Iran war hits 100 days with peace talks stalled
top.description: Trump claims Iran agreed to forgo nuclear weapons, but critics say none of the war's five stated objectives have been achieved.

top.title: Pentagon raises Israel spy threat to highest level
top.description: The Defense Intelligence Agency elevated Israel's counterintelligence designation to "critical" over concerns about surveillance of senior U.S. officials.

top.title: Judge orders ex-CIA officer with $40M in gold to stay jailed
top.description: A Virginia magistrate found David Rush poses a flight risk, calling him uniquely positioned to evade authorities given his intelligence background.

top.title: Trump denies promising 'no new wars,' defends Iran conflict
top.description: In a "Meet the Press" interview airing Sunday, the president rejected claims he pledged to avoid military conflict during his campaign.

top.title: Iran's World Cup squad granted US visas, White House says
top.description: The decision resolves weeks of uncertainty just 10 days before the team's opening match against New Zealand in Los Angeles.

top.title: Putin rejects Zelenskyy's proposal for direct talks
top.description: Russia's president dismissed the call for face-to-face negotiations, saying Moscow wants a comprehensive settlement, not a temporary ceasefire.

top.title: Trump walks out of NBC interview over election fraud clash
top.description: He called anchor Kristen Welker "either crooked or stupid" during a heated exchange about his unsubstantiated California election claims.

top.title: US says 129 ships diverted under naval blockade of Iran
top.description: CENTCOM reported Friday that six vessels have been disabled for noncompliance as the blockade nears its eighth week.

top.title: NASA orders ISS crew to shelter amid worsening air leak
top.description: Astronauts donned spacesuits and boarded their SpaceX capsule Friday as a years-long leak in the Russian segment escalated.

top.title: Trump's top AI adviser Krishnan to leave White House
top.description: Sriram Krishnan, who shaped the administration's deregulatory AI agenda, plans to depart his senior policy role by the end of June.

top.title: US counts nearly 1,000 Hormuz transits since ceasefire
top.description: CENTCOM's tally far exceeds the roughly 650 crossings detected by private ship-tracking services, exposing a major data gap in the world's top oil corridor.

top.title: US adds 172,000 jobs in May, far exceeding forecasts
top.description: The unemployment rate held at 4.3% as payroll growth more than doubled some Wall Street estimates, complicating the case for Fed rate cuts.

top.title: Judge blocks Trump effort to tie SNAP funds to policy conditions
top.description: A federal judge sided with 20 states, issuing a preliminary injunction against attaching immigration and gender-related requirements to food aid funding.

top.title: Iran touts $25B nuclear deal with Russia as US talks stall
top.description: Iran's ambassador revealed the scope of the Hormoz Nuclear Power Plant project as Tehran declared no progress in peace negotiations with Washington.

top.title: House panel requires Pentagon to explain officer firings
top.description: The House Armed Services Committee adopted a measure mandating the defense secretary report to Congress within five days of removing a senior officer.

top.title: Trump pardons ex-Rep. Stephen Buyer for insider trading
top.description: The former Indiana congressman was convicted of securities fraud for illegal stock trades made while consulting for T-Mobile during its Sprint merger.

top.title: Pentagon set to cancel Tomahawk missile sale to Germany
top.description: The expected move would end Germany's bid to buy up to 400 cruise missiles directly, deepening a rift between Washington and Berlin over European defense.

top.title: Hezbollah rejects U.S.-brokered ceasefire as strikes kill 4 in Lebanon
top.description: The group called the deal "absurd" and demanded a full Israeli withdrawal, one day after Israel and Lebanon's government agreed to a new framework.

top.title: Trump holds farmer roundtable in first Wisconsin visit since reelection
top.description: At his first Wisconsin visit of his second term, the president campaigned for a vulnerable House Republican while promising relief for farmers.

top.title: John Bolton to plead guilty in classified documents case
top.description: The former national security adviser agreed to a single charge and a $2.25 million fine, potentially avoiding prison on the original 18-count indictment.

top.title: US job cuts hit highest May total since 2020 as Iran war fallout spreads
top.description: The EU warned 1.3 million jobs are at risk in energy-intensive industries, while US and UK layoffs accelerated sharply.

top.title: Trump signs memo to speed AI use in military operations
top.description: The directive orders agencies to adopt AI from multiple vendors while barring its use for illegal surveillance or censoring speech.

top.title: US strike on alleged drug boat kills 2, pushing toll past 200
top.description: The June 3 strike in the eastern Pacific is the latest in a campaign that has hit more than 60 vessels since September 2025.

top.title: Khamenei says US, Israel shifted to 'hybrid war' after battlefield defeat
top.description: Iran's supreme leader accused adversaries of spreading fear and division, urging national unity at the 37th anniversary of Ayatollah Khomeini's death.

top.title: Federal judge strikes down Trump immigration freeze on 39 countries
top.description: U.S. District Judge McConnell ruled Friday that USCIS unlawfully halted green card, work permit, and asylum processing for nationals of travel-ban countries.

top.title: All 27 EU members approve opening accession talks with Ukraine, Moldova
top.description: Hungary lifted its long-standing veto after reaching a minority rights deal with Kyiv, ending a two-year deadlock on membership negotiations.

top.title: CENTCOM calls Iran's claim of striking US destroyer a lie
top.description: Iran said it hit a US Navy destroyer's command center in the Gulf of Oman; CENTCOM said the claim is false.

top.title: Trump says he wants Pulte to fire intelligence staff
top.description: The president told the Wall Street Journal he views ODNI as "unneeded and possibly too extensive," escalating his overhaul of spy agencies.

top.title: Kim Jong Un calls for 'exponential' nuclear expansion
top.description: North Korea's leader toured a newly operational nuclear material production facility, saying output capacity has more than doubled in five years.

top.title: Rubio says Greenland is part of Denmark 'for now'
top.description: The secretary of state told lawmakers that U.S. talks with Denmark and Greenland on collective defense are "in a good place."

top.title: Bondi told lawmakers Blanche ran Epstein files release
top.description: The 111-page transcript shows the former attorney general invoking executive privilege on any Trump conversations about the case.

top.title: Pentagon watchdog to lead oversight of Iran war
top.description: Inspector General Platte B. Moring III will coordinate reviews across the Pentagon, State Department, and USAID for Operation Epic Fury.

top.title: Proactiv, Dr. Pimple Popper acne creams had benzene 15 times FDA limit
top.description: Bloomberg reported that government lab results showed recalled acne products from Proactiv and SLMD contained benzene levels far exceeding federal safety limits.

top.title: Senate GOP blocks bid to bar Trump's IRS audit immunity fund
top.description: Democrats failed to kill the $1.8 billion "Anti-Weaponization Fund" in a 49-50 vote, as the status of Trump's audit immunity remains unresolved.

top.title: US proposes tariffs on 60 economies over forced labor
top.description: The sweeping Section 301 action covers more than 99% of U.S. imports and marks a major step in rebuilding tariff authority after the Supreme Court struck down IEEPA levies.

top.title: Bolivia's defense minister resigns amid weeks of mass protests
top.description: Marcelo Salinas's departure is the highest-level exit from President Rodrigo Paz's government since unrest erupted in early May.

top.title: Trump's team refuses financial disclosure in BBC lawsuit
top.description: His lawyers sought to halt discovery after the BBC subpoenaed records from nearly 400 entities tied to the Trump Revocable Trust.

top.title: CBS fires Scott Pelley after clash with new '60 Minutes' boss
top.description: The veteran correspondent was terminated Tuesday after accusing Bari Weiss of "murdering" the iconic newsmagazine during a staff meeting.

top.title: Supreme Court lets Alabama eliminate a Black-majority district
top.description: The 6-3 ruling allows a GOP-drawn congressional map for the 2026 midterms, overriding a lower court finding of intentional racial discrimination.

top.title: White House ousts NSC Europe chief in Rubio power play
top.description: The departure of Charles McLaughlin signals Secretary of State Rubio's tightening grip over National Security Council staffing and policy.

top.title: Jeffries says Democrats not focused on Trump impeachment
top.description: The House minority leader told business leaders the party will prioritize affordability and anti-corruption ahead of the November midterms.

top.title: George Santos faces federal probe over Kalshi bets on his own SOTU attendance
top.description: The DOJ and CFTC are investigating whether the disgraced former congressman profited by wagering against his own appearance at the State of the Union.

top.title: Trump confirms calling Netanyahu 'crazy' in call over Lebanon strikes
top.description: The president said he was "a little bit perturbed" by Israel's operations against Hezbollah, which he says are undermining U.S.-Iran peace talks.

top.title: Rubio says Netanyahu's plan to seize 70% of Gaza contradicts US vision
top.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio told lawmakers Tuesday that Israel's expansion directive is not part of Washington's plan for the territory.

top.title: Navy veteran Bennett wins NJ-7 primary to face absent Rep. Kean
top.description: Bennett will challenge Rep. Tom Kean Jr., who has missed over 100 congressional votes during a months-long undisclosed medical absence.

top.title: Trump strips job protections from 8,000 federal workers
top.description: An executive order signed Wednesday converts senior career employees to at-will status, removing their right to appeal disciplinary actions or terminations.

top.title: UNICEF says Iran war delaying lifesaving aid for children
top.description: The agency warned Tuesday that surging transport costs and Gulf shipping disruptions are forcing it to rely on costly air freight to reach children.

top.title: US sanctions two rebel commanders over eastern Congo conflict
top.description: The Treasury Department targeted an M23 intelligence chief and an FDLR special operations commander fueling violence in the DRC.

top.title: House passes war powers resolution to end Iran war
top.description: Four Republicans joined Democrats in the 215-208 vote, delivering a bipartisan rebuke to President Trump over the three-month-old conflict.

top.title: Bahrain bans citizens from traveling to Iran and Iraq
top.description: The ban, citing security tensions, comes as millions of Shiites prepare for pilgrimage season, drawing accusations of sectarian discrimination.

top.title: Rubio says five countries may take stranded Afghan allies
top.description: More than 1,000 Afghans who aided U.S. forces remain stuck at a base in Qatar, but Rubio told senators they won't be resettled in America.

top.title: Iowa farmer Lahn defeats Trump-backed Feenstra in GOP governor primary
top.description: Zach Lahn, aligned with RFK Jr.'s MAHA movement, edged Rep. Randy Feenstra by less than a point in Tuesday's five-candidate race.

top.title: Hegseth blocks Navy officers from admiral promotions
top.description: The defense secretary removed at least seven captains from a one-star admiral list, including two women and two Black men, reports say.

top.title: Takaichi urges Iran to restore free passage through Hormuz
top.description: Japan's PM called on Iran's president to show flexibility in U.S.-Iran talks and reopen the strait to all vessels.

top.title: Trump calls Vance-Rubio 2028 ticket 'unbeatable'
top.description: The president's podcast remarks are his most direct praise yet of the pairing, though he still stopped short of a formal endorsement.

top.title: White House Correspondents' Dinner set for July 24 after shooting postponement
top.description: The WHCA announced the rescheduled date Tuesday, nearly three months after a gunman disrupted the original April 25 event at the Washington Hilton.

top.title: State Dept. to cut Africa visa-processing sites from 50 to 20 hubs
top.description: Secretary of State Rubio approved a directive consolidating consular operations across Africa, forcing applicants in non-hub countries to travel abroad for visas.

top.title: Dr. Oz helms White House briefing, dodges non-health questions
top.description: The CMS administrator announced 160 new drugs on the TrumpRx discount platform while deflecting questions outside his portfolio.

top.title: Pentagon bars journalists from press office, declaring it classified space
top.description: The designation marks the latest escalation in a yearlong campaign to restrict media access to the Defense Department.

top.title: Frederiksen secures third term as Denmark's PM with new centre-left coalition
top.description: The deal ends over two months of deadlocked talks following March's fragmented snap election, which saw 12 parties win seats in parliament.

top.title: Trump endorses Colombia presidential candidate De La Espriella
top.description: The endorsement came via Truth Social on Tuesday as the conservative outsider prepares to face leftist senator Ivan Cepeda in a June 21 runoff.

top.title: Appeals court rules Pentagon transgender troop ban illegal
top.description: A divided D.C. Circuit panel ruled the Pentagon's transgender military ban illegal but allowed the military to keep barring new enlistees.

top.title: Anthropic confidentially files for IPO after $965B valuation
top.description: The Claude AI maker submitted a draft S-1 to the SEC on Monday, joining SpaceX and OpenAI in a historic wave of tech IPOs expected this year.
